goal_spec_json.goal_statement: Sleep a minimum of 6 hours consistently every night

morning_briefing.morning_message:
Hey Ameena. 

You rated yourself at 63 this morning — that's a bit lower than what your body is telling me. Let's start with last night: you got 6.9 hours with 88.6% efficiency, and your deep sleep was actually elevated at 23.3%. That's a recovery-focused sleep, which explains why your HRV shot up to 149 ms — that's **excellent** parasympathetic recovery, 1.7 standard deviations above your baseline. Your body is in a strong rest-and-repair state.

Yesterday, however, was completely sedentary — zero minutes of moderate activity and nearly 13 hours sitting. That high recovery coinciding with low activity is interesting: your physiology got a break, but the inactivity might be contributing to that 'neutral-to-slightly-positive' feeling you reported. There's also that persistent gap where you feel lower than your body's signals suggest, and we're missing your subjective check-in and nutrition data from yesterday to complete the picture.

The good news is your recovery foundation is solid. Today is about bridging that feeling gap and gently reintroducing rhythm. Your actions are designed to build awareness from the moment you wake up and protect your energy throughout the day. 

Even with the late schedule, your sleep quality held strong — let's build on that resilience today.

assumptions: User is observing Ramadan (based on late meal timing patterns)
High workload and tight schedule remain active constraints
Missing checkin/journal data indicates user may be time-constrained or disengaged
Nutrition gaps may reflect fasting patterns or logging fatigue

evidence_used.nutrition_gap: Zero meals logged, nutrition_confidence_day 0, protein_g_day null, total_kcal_day null
evidence_used.recent_trends: Sleep onset time increasing (+9.86 slope_slow), wake time increasing (+17.52 slope_slow), stress rising (+1.87 slope_slow), HRV improving (+2.90 slope_slow)
evidence_used.stress_profile: Stress score 77 (high), coping capacity 61 (moderate), stress_minus_coping +16 indicates coping deficit
evidence_used.energy_mismatch: Overall score 63 (normal energy_mode) but subjective energy_score 28 (low), focus_score 28 (low)
evidence_used.data_missingness: checkin, journal, nutrition data missing; confidence scores 0 for those domains
evidence_used.mismatch_pattern: gap_today -5 (overall < proxy), residual signature shows overall_lt_proxy bias
evidence_used.volatility_signals: Global volatility index 0.4953 (volatile); sleep_duration_hours, stress_score, energy_score all volatile
evidence_used.sleep_goal_alignment: Sleep duration 6.9h below 7h target, efficiency 88.65% good, wake ratio 11.13% moderate

soft_constraints: caution: high stress (77) with rising trend (+0.88 regime shift) and elevated coping-stress gap (stress_minus_coping +16)
caution: low subjective energy (28) despite normal energy_mode
friction-reduction: nutrition logging is absent (meal_count_day=0, confidence=0) - consider quick protein addition at Iftar
recovery caution: sleep debt pattern (sleep_duration_hours 6.9 below goal of 7+; recent volatility)

generated_actions.title: When you feel mental fog in the afternoon, do a 90-second '5-4-3-2-1' sensory reset
generated_actions.rationale: Yesterday's stress was high (77) with a rising trend (+1.87 slope_slow) and elevated coping-stress gap (+16). You're reporting low subjective energy (28) despite good physiological recovery. This implementation intention targets the mental fog and tension that often accompany high stress, providing an immediate, low-effort coping tool to interrupt the stress spiral and bridge the subjective-objective gap.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you use the 5-4-3-2-1 sensory reset when you felt mental fog or tension this afternoon?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports completing at least one intentional sensory grounding break when mental fog or tension was noticed.

assumptions: You can take a brief mental break when tension arises
generated_actions.description: If you notice mental fog or tension building this afternoon, pause immediately. Name: 5 things you see, 4 things you touch, 3 things you hear, 2 things you smell, 1 thing you taste. Breathe slowly while doing this.

must_avoid: continuing the stressful task during the break

generated_actions.title: Add a protein source to your first meal after sunset tonight
generated_actions.rationale: Yesterday showed complete absence of nutrition data (meal_count_day=0, confidence=0), continuing a declining trend during Ramadan. This friction-reduction action directly addresses the 'nutrition_gap' risk flag by making protein addition automatic and simple, supporting energy needs while also encouraging meal logging to improve data quality.

signal_refs.label: at least one meal logged today

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you intentionally add extra protein to your first meal after sunset tonight?
evaluation.success_definition: At least one meal is logged today (ending the zero-meal streak) AND user reports consciously enhancing their evening meal with protein.

assumptions: You are observing Ramadan and will break your fast at sunset
You have access to protein sources
generated_actions.description: When you break your fast tonight, consciously include one palm-sized protein portion—such as an extra boiled egg, a scoop of Greek yogurt, or a piece of chicken—to boost today's intake.

generated_actions.title: After waking up, state one word for your current energy level out loud
generated_actions.rationale: You continue to show a subjective-objective mismatch (gap_today -5, overall_lt_proxy bias) with low subjective energy (28) despite excellent physiological recovery (HRV 149.3, proxy 68). This tiny habit-stacked action promotes non-judgmental awareness of your actual energy state, gently bridging the cognitive-emotional gap and addressing the 'mismatch_pattern' risk flag without demanding cognitive effort.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you state one energy word out loud after waking up today?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports completing the brief energy acknowledgment exercise upon waking.

assumptions: You will wake up and speak out loud
generated_actions.description: Right after you get out of bed today, say one word that describes your energy level (e.g., 'slow', 'neutral', 'heavy'). Just acknowledge it without judgment.

generated_actions.title: At 11:30 PM, start a 15-minute 'no-screen wind-down' before bed
generated_actions.rationale: Your sleep timing has been very late (onset ~5:44 AM, wake ~1:31 PM) with both onset and wake times increasing (+9.86 and +17.52 slope_slow). This action directly supports your sleep goal domain and today's 'sleep_schedule_consistency_with_recovery_focus' priority by creating a consistent pre-sleep routine that protects against digital stimulation, aiming to gradually stabilize your sleep timing.

evaluation.completion_prompt: Did you initiate a 15-minute screen-free wind-down starting at 11:30 PM tonight?
evaluation.success_definition: User reports starting a screen-free, low-stimulation wind-down at or close to 11:30 PM for at least 15 minutes.

assumptions: Your schedule allows starting wind-down by 11:30 PM
You have access to low-light activities
generated_actions.description: When 11:30 PM arrives, put away all screens (phone, computer, TV). Spend 15 minutes on low-stimulation activities like light tidying, reading a physical book, or gentle stretching before getting into bed.
